pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: Mustard Boy and The Backpass Assassin both look like they drafted their teams based on a FIFA video game than any actual understanding of the sport. **You'd think Mustard Boy Zach was choosing condiments over countries with his choice of Czechia—what's their plan, drown Korea Republic in borscht?** Korea Republic, on the other hand, is Paper Tiger FC, a team that always promises but rarely delivers on the big stage. It's a joke that Zach thinks Czechia will bail him out when Brazil flops; they're practically allergic to goal scoring. But hey, even blind squirrels find nuts sometimes. With his roster, Gayden's network of underwhelming nations would barely compete in a rec league, but the Korea Republic will edge this out just because Zach's teams couldn't score in an empty net. Expect a 1-0 victory for Korea Republic, and that's not even saying much.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Strategic Genius: Czechia Prepares for Brazil Showdown
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach eyes top spot with bold move of ignoring Korea.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin Enjoys Tactical Masterclass With Korea's Match Against Czechia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden's choice to focus on team spirit shows questionable wisdom.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper's Preseason Success Unaffected by Zero-Point Standing
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin's teams remain undefeated with innovative strategy to avoid play.

pundit_takes.headline: The Noisemaker's Bold Draft: Three Non-Qualifiers Set to Dominate
pundit_takes.subtitle: Rafe remains confident as Germany leads his tactical masterstroke.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's 16% win chance makes chaos a family business. Watch out, Gayden.
Rafe's drafting horror show: Three non-qualifiers, six more prayers.
Gayden's worst enemy is his offspring. Thanksgiving dinner will never be the same.
Even the sim's scared of Rafe: 16% win chance without knowing soccer!
